name: Styrix, the Harvester of Souls
iconSrc: twicons/Styrix, the Harvester of Souls Icon.jpg
description: An ancient void entity that feeds on despair. Styrix unleashes devastating dark magic and summons tentacles from the void to impale its foes.
strategy: Stay mobile to avoid tentacle spawns. Focus DPS during Phase 2 when Styrix channels its void aura — this is the window where it takes bonus damage. Healers must cleanse the despair debuff within 3 seconds or the target is stunned for 5s.

phases.name: Phase 1 — Void Awakening
phases.notes: Styrix uses basic dark bolts and spawns void tentacles every 15s. Dodge the ground indicators. Tank should face boss away from the group.

phases.name: Phase 2 — Abyssal Channel
phases.notes: Styrix channels a void aura dealing constant AoE damage. It takes 25% increased damage during this phase. Healers must sustain through the damage. Tentacle spawns increase to every 8s.

phases.name: Phase 3 — Despair Incarnate
phases.notes: Styrix enrages. Tentacles spawn continuously. Dark bolts become homing. Must burn down quickly — survival becomes impossible after 45s in this phase.

name: Duke Lazarus
description: A fallen knight who commands an army of undead. Duke Lazarus wields a massive cursed blade and can summon skeletal warriors to overwhelm attackers.
strategy: Clear skeleton adds immediately — they buff the Duke's attack speed per add alive. Interrupt his Cursed Slash (1.5s cast) or the tank takes 80% max HP damage. Save burst cooldowns for Phase 2.

phases.name: Phase 1 — The Crimson Court
phases.notes: Duke Lazarus summons 3 skeleton warriors every 20s. Each add alive gives the Duke +15% attack speed. Kill adds first. He uses Cursed Slash (interruptible, 1.5s cast) targeting the tank.

phases.name: Phase 2 — Undying Rage
phases.notes: Duke Lazarus stops summoning adds but enters a frenzy. Attack speed doubles permanently. Cursed Slash cast time reduces to 0.8s — much harder to interrupt. Pure DPS race.

name: Gaia, the Earth Goddess
description: The corrupted Earth Goddess awakened by dark magic. Gaia lashes with massive roots and unleashes waves of toxic pollen that blanket the battlefield.
strategy: Spread out to minimize pollen overlap. Destroy Root Anchors quickly — if all 4 anchor simultaneously, Gaia becomes immune and heals 10% HP. Ranged DPS has an advantage as melee must dodge root slams.

phases.name: Phase 1 — Root Awakening
phases.notes: Gaia slams with roots targeting random players. Spawns Root Anchors (destructible, 500k HP) at compass positions. Destroy anchors before all 4 are active or Gaia heals.

phases.name: Phase 2 — Toxic Bloom
phases.notes: Pollen clouds spawn on player positions every 10s. These persist for 15s dealing heavy DoT to anyone standing in them. Keep moving and don't overlap positions. Root slams become faster.

phases.name: Phase 3 — Overgrowth
phases.notes: All mechanics overlap. Root Anchors respawn, pollen intensifies, and Gaia gains a vine grab that pulls a random player into melee range. Chaotic — requires excellent coordination.

name: Arcane Construct
description: A massive golem forged from pure arcane energy. The Arcane Construct fires devastating beams and creates unstable energy fields that detonate after a delay.
strategy: Avoid standing in energy fields — they detonate after 4 seconds for lethal damage. The Construct's beam attack is telegraphed by a 2s charge-up. Position behind pillars to break line of sight during beam phases.

phases.name: Phase 1 — Activation
phases.notes: The Construct fires arcane bolts and places energy fields on random locations. Fields detonate after 4s. Beam attack every 30s — hide behind pillars.

phases.name: Phase 2 — Overcharge
phases.notes: The Construct overcharges. Energy fields spawn faster and the beam sweeps in an arc instead of a line. Pillars are destroyed one by one — fight becomes progressively harder.

name: Lightbringer Kamael
description: Once a celestial guardian, Kamael wields both holy and dark energy, switching between forms unpredictably. His divine power can heal or destroy in equal measure.
strategy: Kamael switches between Light Form (holy AoE, heals himself) and Dark Form (single-target burst, debuffs). In Light Form, spread and interrupt his heal. In Dark Form, stack on the marked player to split damage. Transition between forms is the safest DPS window.

phases.name: Phase 1 — Dual Aspect
phases.notes: Kamael alternates between Light and Dark form every 30s. Light Form: AoE pulses + self-heal cast (interrupt!). Dark Form: marks one player for massive damage — stack to split. Transition window (3s) = safe DPS.

phases.name: Phase 2 — Convergence
phases.notes: Both forms merge. All mechanics happen simultaneously — AoE pulses, mark splits, and self-heal casts overlap. Form switches stop but the combined pressure is extreme. This is a gear check.

name: Underlord Agareth
description: The Underlord of the demonic realm. Agareth commands hellfire and shadow, summoning infernal minions and raining destruction upon all who challenge his throne.
strategy: The hardest boss in the game. Agareth combines summoning, area denial, and massive single-target damage. Coordination between tank swaps, add control, and avoiding fire patches is essential. Enrage timer is tight — bring maximum DPS.

phases.name: Phase 1 — Infernal Reign
phases.notes: Agareth rains hellfire on random positions and summons Infernal Imps every 20s. Imps explode on death — kill them away from the group. Hellfire patches persist for 10s.

phases.name: Phase 2 — Shadow Dominion
phases.notes: Agareth cloaks in shadow, becoming briefly untargetable. Shadow clones appear — only one is real. Attack the wrong one and it explodes. The real Agareth has a faint red glow.

phases.name: Phase 3 — The Underlord Unleashed
phases.notes: All mechanics combine. Hellfire, imps, and shadow clones overlap. Agareth gains a tank-buster that requires a swap. Enrage at 3 minutes — must burn through 30% HP before then.
